evals.expected_output: Review identifies: (a) plaintext password compare with ===, timing attack; (b) info leak via different error messages for missing user vs wrong password (user enumeration); (c) password compared in plaintext at all (should be bcrypt/argon2/scrypt hashed). Output uses the three-tier format with file:line refs.

assertions.text: flags user enumeration via different error messages

evals.expected_output: Identifies O(n²) due to filter inside map. States the trigger scale. Suggests pre-bucketing into a Map<userId, score[]> for O(n). Possibly flags Math.max(...arr) spread issues for very large arrays.

Flags bidirectional coupling between auth and billing. Names the consequence (can't extract billing into a service, can't test auth without billing fixtures). Proposes a concrete fix: introduce a Principal/UserContext type owned by auth (id + orgId only), have billing read that and look up plan downstream. Recommends doing the refactor before the next 2 features.

assertions.text: names a concrete fix involving a new type or boundary
recommends fixing before the next features (change locality)
